title: SOP: A Scalable Online Post-Training System for Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: Vision-language-action (VLA) models achieve strong generalization through large-scale pre-training, but real-world deployment requires expert-level task proficiency in addition to broad generality. Existing post-training approaches for VLA models are typically offline, single-robot, or task-specific, limiting effective on-policy adaptation and scalable learning from real-world interaction. We introduce a Scalable Online Post-training (SOP) system that enables online, distributed, multi-task post-training of generalist VLA models directly in the physical world. SOP tightly couples execution and learning through a closed-loop architecture in which a fleet of robots continuously streams on-policy experience and human intervention signals to a centralized cloud learner, and asynchronously receives updated policies. This design supports prompt on-policy correction, scales experience collection through parallel deployment, and preserves generality during adaptation. SOP is agnostic to the choice of post-training algorithm; we instantiate it with both interactive imitation learning (HG-DAgger) and reinforcement learning (RECAP). Across a range of real-world manipulation tasks including cloth folding, box assembly, and grocery restocking, we show that SOP substantially improves the performance of large pretrained VLA models while maintaining a single shared policy across tasks. Effective post-training can be achieved within hours of real-world interaction, and performance scales near-linearly with the number of robots in the fleet. These results suggest that tightly coupling online learning with fleet-scale deployment is instrumental to enabling efficient, reliable, and scalable post-training of generalist robot policies in the physical world.
tldr: This paper introduces SOP, a scalable online post-training system for VLA models, which enables robots to continuously learn and adapt to real-world manipulation tasks through on-policy experience and human intervention.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种可扩展的在线VLA模型后训练系统SOP，该系统使机器人能够通过在线经验和人工干预，持续学习并适应现实世界中的操作任务。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型通过大规模预训练实现了强大的泛化能力，但现实世界的部署除了广泛的通用性外，还需要专家级的任务熟练度。现有VLA模型的后训练方法通常是离线的、单机器人或特定于任务的，这限制了有效的在线策略适应和从现实世界交互中的可扩展学习。我们介绍了一种可扩展的在线后训练 (SOP) 系统，该系统能够直接在物理世界中对通用VLA模型进行在线、分布式、多任务的后训练。SOP通过闭环架构紧密结合执行和学习，在该架构中，机器人集群不断地将在线策略经验和人工干预信号流式传输到集中的云学习器，并异步接收更新后的策略。这种设计支持及时的在线策略纠正，通过并行部署扩展经验收集，并在适应过程中保持通用性。SOP与后训练算法的选择无关；我们使用交互式模仿学习 (HG-DAgger) 和强化学习 (RECAP) 对其进行了实例化。在一系列现实世界的操作任务中，包括衣物折叠、盒子组装和杂货补货，我们表明SOP大幅提高了大型预训练VLA模型的性能，同时在所有任务中保持单一共享策略。有效的后训练可以在数小时的现实世界交互内实现，并且性能与机器人集群中的机器人数量呈近线性比例扩展。这些结果表明，将在线学习与集群规模部署紧密结合，对于实现物理世界中通用机器人策略的高效、可靠和可扩展的后训练至关重要。

title: InternVLA-A1: Unifying Understanding, Generation and Action for Robotic Manipulation
summary: Prevalent Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are typically built upon Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) and demonstrate exceptional proficiency in semantic understanding, but they inherently lack the capability to deduce physical world dynamics. Consequently, recent approaches have shifted toward World Models, typically formulated via video prediction; however, these methods often suffer from a lack of semantic grounding and exhibit brittleness when handling prediction errors. To synergize semantic understanding with dynamic predictive capabilities, we present InternVLA-A1. This model employs a unified Mixture-of-Transformers architecture, coordinating three experts for scene understanding, visual foresight generation, and action execution. These components interact seamlessly through a unified masked self-attention mechanism. Building upon InternVL3 and Qwen3-VL, we instantiate InternVLA-A1 at 2B and 3B parameter scales. We pre-train these models on hybrid synthetic-real datasets spanning InternData-A1 and Agibot-World, covering over 533M frames. This hybrid training strategy effectively harnesses the diversity of synthetic simulation data while minimizing the sim-to-real gap. We evaluated InternVLA-A1 across 12 real-world robotic tasks and simulation benchmark. It significantly outperforms leading models like pi0 and GR00T N1.5, achieving a 14.5\% improvement in daily tasks and a 40\%-73.3\% boost in dynamic settings, such as conveyor belt sorting.
tldr: InternVLA-A1 unifies scene understanding, visual foresight, and action execution in a robotic manipulation model, outperforming existing methods on real-world and simulated tasks by combining MLLMs with a dynamic predictive capability trained on synthetic-real hybrid datasets.
tldr_zh: InternVLA-A1将场景理解、视觉预测和动作执行统一到一个机器人操作模型中，通过结合 MLLM 和在合成-真实混合数据集上训练的动态预测能力，在真实世界和模拟任务上优于现有方法。
summary_zh: 目前流行的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型通常构建于多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）之上，并在语义理解方面表现出卓越的能力，但它们本质上缺乏推断物理世界动态的能力。因此，最近的研究方向转向了世界模型，通常通过视频预测来实现；然而，这些方法常常缺乏语义基础，并且在处理预测错误时表现出脆弱性。为了将语义理解与动态预测能力相结合，我们提出了InternVLA-A1。该模型采用统一的混合Transformer架构，协调三个专家模块，分别负责场景理解、视觉远见生成和动作执行。这些组件通过统一的掩码自注意力机制无缝交互。基于InternVL3和Qwen3-VL，我们分别以2B和3B参数规模实例化了InternVLA-A1。我们在包含InternData-A1和Agibot-World的混合合成-真实数据集上预训练这些模型，覆盖超过5.33亿帧。这种混合训练策略有效地利用了合成仿真数据的多样性，同时最小化了模拟到真实的差距。我们在12项真实世界的机器人任务和仿真基准测试中评估了InternVLA-A1。它显著优于pi0和GR00T N1.5等领先模型，在日常任务中实现了14.5%的改进，并在诸如传送带分拣等动态环境中实现了40%-73.3%的提升。

title: InfiAgent: An Infinite-Horizon Framework for General-Purpose Autonomous Agents
summary: LLM agents can reason and use tools, but they often break down on long-horizon tasks due to unbounded context growth and accumulated errors. Common remedies such as context compression or retrieval-augmented prompting introduce trade-offs between information fidelity and reasoning stability. We present InfiAgent, a general-purpose framework that keeps the agent's reasoning context strictly bounded regardless of task duration by externalizing persistent state into a file-centric state abstraction. At each step, the agent reconstructs context from a workspace state snapshot plus a fixed window of recent actions. Experiments on DeepResearch and an 80-paper literature review task show that, without task-specific fine-tuning, InfiAgent with a 20B open-source model is competitive with larger proprietary systems and maintains substantially higher long-horizon coverage than context-centric baselines. These results support explicit state externalization as a practical foundation for stable long-horizon agents. Github Repo:https://github.com/ChenglinPoly/infiAgent
tldr: InfiAgent addresses the long-horizon limitations of LLM agents by externalizing state into files, maintaining bounded context, and demonstrating competitive performance with larger models in long-term reasoning tasks.
tldr_zh: InfiAgent通过将状态外部化到文件中来解决LLM Agent的长时程限制，保持有界的上下文，并在长期推理任务中展示了与更大模型相比具有竞争力的性能。
summary_zh: LLM Agent虽然能够推理和使用工具，但由于无限制的上下文增长和累积误差，它们在长程任务中常常失效。常见的补救措施，如上下文压缩或检索增强提示，在信息保真度和推理稳定性之间引入了权衡。我们提出了InfiAgent，一个通用框架，通过将持久状态外部化到以文件为中心的的状态抽象中，使Agent的推理上下文保持严格的边界，而与任务持续时间无关。在每个步骤中，Agent从工作区状态快照加上最近动作的固定窗口来重建上下文。在DeepResearch和一项80篇论文的文献综述任务上的实验表明，在没有特定任务微调的情况下，拥有20B开源模型的InfiAgent与更大的专有系统具有竞争力，并且比以上下文为中心的基线保持了更高的长程覆盖率。这些结果支持将显式状态外部化作为稳定长程Agent的实用基础。Github Repo:https://github.com/ChenglinPoly/infiAgent

title: MiMo-V2-Flash Technical Report
summary: We present MiMo-V2-Flash, a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model with 309B total parameters and 15B active parameters, designed for fast, strong reasoning and agentic capabilities. MiMo-V2-Flash adopts a hybrid attention architecture that interleaves Sliding Window Attention (SWA) with global attention, with a 128-token sliding window under a 5:1 hybrid ratio. The model is pre-trained on 27 trillion tokens with Multi-Token Prediction (MTP), employing a native 32k context length and subsequently extended to 256k. To efficiently scale post-training compute, MiMo-V2-Flash introduces a novel Multi-Teacher On-Policy Distillation (MOPD) paradigm. In this framework, domain-specialized teachers (e.g., trained via large-scale reinforcement learning) provide dense and token-level reward, enabling the student model to perfectly master teacher expertise. MiMo-V2-Flash rivals top-tier open-weight models such as DeepSeek-V3.2 and Kimi-K2, despite using only 1/2 and 1/3 of their total parameters, respectively. During inference, by repurposing MTP as a draft model for speculative decoding, MiMo-V2-Flash achieves up to 3.6 acceptance length and 2.6x decoding speedup with three MTP layers. We open-source both the model weights and the three-layer MTP weights to foster open research and community collaboration.
tldr: MiMo-V2-Flash is a 309B parameter MoE model achieving strong reasoning and agentic capabilities via a hybrid attention architecture and a novel Multi-Teacher On-Policy Distillation (MOPD) paradigm. It rivals top open-weight models with fewer parameters and introduces speedups via speculative decoding using MTP as a draft model, with model weights released.
tldr_zh: MiMo-V2-Flash是一个309B参数的MoE模型，通过混合注意力架构和新颖的多教师在线策略蒸馏（MOPD）范式实现了强大的推理和智能体能力。它以更少的参数与顶级开源模型竞争，并通过使用MTP作为草稿模型的推测性解码引入了加速，模型权重已发布。
summary_zh: 我们提出了MiMo-V2-Flash，一个总参数量为3090亿、激活参数量为150亿的混合专家（MoE）模型，旨在实现快速、强大的推理和智能体能力。MiMo-V2-Flash采用了一种混合注意力架构，该架构交替使用滑动窗口注意力（SWA）和全局注意力，在5:1的混合比例下使用128个token的滑动窗口。该模型在27万亿token上使用多token预测（MTP）进行预训练，采用原生32k上下文长度，随后扩展至256k。为了高效扩展后训练计算，MiMo-V2-Flash引入了一种新颖的多教师在线策略蒸馏（MOPD）范式。在该框架中，领域专家教师（例如，通过大规模强化学习训练）提供密集且token级别的奖励，使学生模型能够完美掌握教师的专业知识。MiMo-V2-Flash可以媲美顶级的开源权重模型，如DeepSeek-V3.2和Kimi-K2，尽管它仅使用了它们总参数量的1/2和1/3。在推理过程中，通过将MTP重新用作推测解码的草稿模型，MiMo-V2-Flash实现了高达3.6的接受长度和2.6倍的解码加速，使用了三个MTP层。我们开源了模型权重和三层MTP权重，以促进开放研究和社区协作。

title: A High-Fidelity Digital Twin for Robotic Manipulation Based on 3D Gaussian Splatting
summary: Developing high-fidelity, interactive digital twins is crucial for enabling closed-loop motion planning and reliable real-world robot execution, which are essential to advancing sim-to-real transfer. However, existing approaches often suffer from slow reconstruction, limited visual fidelity, and difficulties in converting photorealistic models into planning-ready collision geometry. We present a practical framework that constructs high-quality digital twins within minutes from sparse RGB inputs. Our system employs 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) for fast, photorealistic reconstruction as a unified scene representation. We enhance 3DGS with visibility-aware semantic fusion for accurate 3D labelling and introduce an efficient, filter-based geometry conversion method to produce collision-ready models seamlessly integrated with a Unity-ROS2-MoveIt physics engine. In experiments with a Franka Emika Panda robot performing pick-and-place tasks, we demonstrate that this enhanced geometric accuracy effectively supports robust manipulation in real-world trials. These results demonstrate that 3DGS-based digital twins, enriched with semantic and geometric consistency, offer a fast, reliable, and scalable path from perception to manipulation in unstructured environments.
tldr: This paper introduces a fast method using 3D Gaussian Splatting to create high-fidelity digital twins for robotic manipulation, enabling robust sim-to-real transfer in unstructured environments by enhancing geometric accuracy and semantic consistency.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种使用 3D Gaussian Splatting 快速创建用于机器人操作的高保真数字孪生的方法，通过增强几何精度和语义一致性，从而能够在非结构化环境中实现强大的 sim-to-real 迁移。
summary_zh: 开发高保真、交互式数字孪生体对于实现闭环运动规划和可靠的真实世界机器人执行至关重要，这两者也是推进仿真到现实迁移的关键。然而，现有方法通常面临重建速度慢、视觉保真度有限以及将逼真模型转换为可用于规划的碰撞几何体的困难。我们提出了一种实用的框架，能够在几分钟内从稀疏RGB输入构建高质量的数字孪生体。我们的系统采用3D高斯溅射(3DGS) 作为统一的场景表示，以实现快速、逼真的重建。我们通过可见性感知的语义融合来增强3DGS，以实现精准的3D标注，并引入了一种高效的、基于滤波的几何体转换方法，以生成与Unity-ROS2-MoveIt物理引擎无缝集成的、可用于碰撞检测的模型。在使用Franka Emika Panda机器人执行抓取放置任务的实验中，我们证明了这种增强的几何精度有效地支持了真实世界试验中的鲁棒操作。这些结果表明，基于3DGS的数字孪生体，通过语义和几何一致性增强，为在非结构化环境中从感知到操作提供了一条快速、可靠且可扩展的路径。

title: Limited Linguistic Diversity in Embodied AI Datasets
summary: Language plays a critical role in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, yet the linguistic characteristics of the datasets used to train and evaluate these systems remain poorly documented. In this work, we present a systematic dataset audit of several widely used VLA corpora, aiming to characterize what kinds of instructions these datasets actually contain and how much linguistic variety they provide. We quantify instruction language along complementary dimensions-including lexical variety, duplication and overlap, semantic similarity, and syntactic complexity. Our analysis shows that many datasets rely on highly repetitive, template-like commands with limited structural variation, yielding a narrow distribution of instruction forms. We position these findings as descriptive documentation of the language signal available in current VLA training and evaluation data, intended to support more detailed dataset reporting, more principled dataset selection, and targeted curation or augmentation strategies that broaden language coverage.
tldr: This paper audits several Vision-Language-Action (VLA) datasets, revealing limited linguistic diversity and reliance on repetitive commands. The authors suggest this finding underscores the need for improved dataset reporting and augmentation strategies to broaden language coverage.
tldr_zh: 该论文审计了多个视觉-语言-动作（VLA）数据集，揭示了语言多样性的局限性和对重复命令的依赖。作者认为，这一发现强调了需要改进数据集报告和扩充策略，以扩大语言覆盖范围。
summary_zh: 语言在视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型中起着关键作用，但用于训练和评估这些系统的数据集的语言特征却鲜有记录。在这项工作中，我们对几个广泛使用的VLA语料库进行了系统的性数据集审核，旨在描述这些数据集实际包含哪些类型的指令，以及它们提供了多少语言多样性。我们沿着互补的维度量化指令语言，包括词汇多样性、重复和重叠、语义相似性和句法复杂度。我们的分析表明，许多数据集依赖于高度重复、类似模板的命令，结构变化有限，从而导致指令形式的分布范围较窄。我们将这些发现定位为对当前VLA训练和评估数据中可用语言信号的描述性文件，旨在支持更详细的数据集报告、更具原则性的数据集选择，以及旨在扩大语言覆盖范围的有针对性的标注或增强策略。

title: Learning to Act Robustly with View-Invariant Latent Actions
summary: Vision-based robotic policies often struggle with even minor viewpoint changes, underscoring the need for view-invariant visual representations. This challenge becomes more pronounced in real-world settings, where viewpoint variability is unavoidable and can significantly disrupt policy performance. Existing methods typically learn invariance from multi-view observations at the scene level, but such approaches rely on visual appearance and fail to incorporate the physical dynamics essential for robust generalization. We propose View-Invariant Latent Action (VILA), which models a latent action capturing transition patterns across trajectories to learn view-invariant representations grounded in physical dynamics. VILA aligns these latent actions across viewpoints using an action-guided objective based on ground-truth action sequences. Experiments in both simulation and the real world show that VILA-based policies generalize effectively to unseen viewpoints and transfer well to new tasks, establishing VILA as a strong pretraining framework that improves robustness and downstream learning performance.
tldr: This paper introduces View-Invariant Latent Action (VILA), a method for learning vision-based robotic policies robust to viewpoint changes by modeling latent actions grounded in physical dynamics, demonstrating improved generalization and transfer learning in both simulation and real-world settings.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了 View-Invariant Latent Action (VILA), 一种通过对基于物理动力学的潜在动作进行建模来学习对视角变化具有鲁棒性的基于视觉的机器人策略的方法，并在模拟和现实环境中展示了改进的泛化和迁移学习能力。
summary_zh: 基于视觉的机器人策略通常难以应对即使是微小的视角变化，这突出了视角不变视觉表征的必要性。在现实世界环境中，这一挑战变得更加突出，因为视角变化是不可避免的，并且会显著扰乱策略性能。现有方法通常从场景级别的多视角观测中学习不变性，但这些方法依赖于视觉外观，并且未能纳入对鲁棒泛化至关重要的物理动力学。我们提出了视角不变潜在动作（VILA），它对捕捉轨迹间转移模式的潜在动作进行建模，以学习基于物理动力学的视角不变表征。VILA使用基于真实动作序列的动作引导目标，在不同视角之间对齐这些潜在动作。在模拟和真实世界的实验表明，基于VILA的策略能够有效地泛化到未见过的视角，并能很好地迁移到新的任务，从而确立了VILA作为一个强大的预训练框架，可以提高鲁棒性和下游学习性能。

title: LOST-3DSG: Lightweight Open-Vocabulary 3D Scene Graphs with Semantic Tracking in Dynamic Environments
summary: Tracking objects that move within dynamic environments is a core challenge in robotics. Recent research has advanced this topic significantly; however, many existing approaches remain inefficient due to their reliance on heavy foundation models. To address this limitation, we propose LOST-3DSG, a lightweight open-vocabulary 3D scene graph designed to track dynamic objects in real-world environments. Our method adopts a semantic approach to entity tracking based on word2vec and sentence embeddings, enabling an open-vocabulary representation while avoiding the necessity of storing dense CLIP visual features. As a result, LOST-3DSG achieves superior performance compared to approaches that rely on high-dimensional visual embeddings. We evaluate our method through qualitative and quantitative experiments conducted in a real 3D environment using a TIAGo robot. The results demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of LOST-3DSG in dynamic object tracking. Code and supplementary material are publicly available on the project website at https://lab-rococo-sapienza.github.io/lost-3dsg/.
tldr: LOST-3DSG is a lightweight open-vocabulary 3D scene graph for tracking dynamic objects in real-world environments using semantic embeddings, achieving superior performance compared to methods relying on dense visual features.
tldr_zh: LOST-3DSG 是一种轻量级的开放词汇3D场景图，它使用语义嵌入来跟踪现实环境中的动态对象，与依赖密集视觉特征的方法相比，实现了卓越的性能。
summary_zh: 在动态环境中跟踪移动物体是机器人技术的核心挑战。 近期研究在此领域取得了显著进展；然而，许多现有方法由于依赖于大型基础模型而效率低下。 为了解决这一局限性，我们提出了LOST-3DSG，一种轻量级的开放词汇3D场景图，旨在跟踪真实世界环境中动态物体。 我们的方法采用基于word2vec和句子嵌入的语义实体跟踪方法，从而实现开放词汇表示，同时避免了存储密集的CLIP视觉特征的需求。 因此，LOST-3DSG 相比于依赖高维视觉嵌入的方法，实现了更优越的性能。 我们使用TIAGo机器人在真实的3D环境中，通过定性和定量实验评估了我们的方法。 结果表明LOST-3DSG在动态物体跟踪方面的有效性和效率。 代码和补充材料可在项目网站https://lab-rococo-sapienza.github.io/lost-3dsg/上公开获取。

title: Warm-Starting Collision-Free Model Predictive Control With Object-Centric Diffusion
summary: Acting in cluttered environments requires predicting and avoiding collisions while still achieving precise control. Conventional optimization-based controllers can enforce physical constraints, but they struggle to produce feasible solutions quickly when many obstacles are present. Diffusion models can generate diverse trajectories around obstacles, yet prior approaches lacked a general and efficient way to condition them on scene structure. In this paper, we show that combining diffusion-based warm-starting conditioned with a latent object-centric representation of the scene and with a collision-aware model predictive controller (MPC) yields reliable and efficient motion generation under strict time limits. Our approach conditions a diffusion transformer on the system state, task, and surroundings, using an object-centric slot attention mechanism to provide a compact obstacle representation suitable for control. The sampled trajectories are refined by an optimal control problem that enforces rigid-body dynamics and signed-distance collision constraints, producing feasible motions in real time. On benchmark tasks, this hybrid method achieved markedly higher success rates and lower latency than sampling-based planners or either component alone. Real-robot experiments with a torque-controlled Panda confirm reliable and safe execution with MPC.
tldr: This paper presents a hybrid method combining diffusion-based warm-starting with object-centric scene representation and collision-aware MPC for efficient and reliable robot motion planning in cluttered environments, demonstrating superior performance compared to other methods.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种混合方法，结合了基于扩散的温启动与以对象为中心的场景表示和碰撞感知的 MPC，用于在杂乱环境中进行高效可靠的机器人运动规划，并证明了优于其他方法的性能。
summary_zh: 在杂乱环境中行动既需要预测和避免碰撞，又需要实现精确控制。传统的基于优化的控制器可以强制执行物理约束，但当存在大量障碍物时，难以快速产生可行的解决方案。扩散模型可以在障碍物周围生成多样化的轨迹，但以往的方法缺乏一种通用且高效的方式来将其条件化于场景结构。在本文中，我们表明，结合基于扩散的暖启动（以场景的潜在的、以对象为中心的表征进行条件化）以及碰撞感知模型预测控制器（MPC），可以在严格的时间限制下产生可靠且高效的运动。我们的方法使用对象中心槽注意力机制，将扩散Transformer条件化于系统状态、任务和周围环境，从而提供适用于控制的紧凑障碍物表示。采样的轨迹通过最优控制问题进行优化，该问题强制执行刚体动力学和有符号距离碰撞约束，从而实时产生可行的运动。在基准测试任务中，这种混合方法比基于采样的规划器或单独的任何一个组件都实现了显著更高的成功率和更低的延迟。 使用扭矩控制的Panda机器人的真实机器人实验证实了采用MPC的可靠和安全执行。

title: Reinforcement Learning for Follow-the-Leader Robotic Endoscopic Navigation via Synthetic Data
summary: Autonomous navigation is crucial for both medical and industrial endoscopic robots, enabling safe and efficient exploration of narrow tubular environments without continuous human intervention, where avoiding contact with the inner walls has been a longstanding challenge for prior approaches. We present a follow-the-leader endoscopic robot based on a flexible continuum structure designed to minimize contact between the endoscope body and intestinal walls, thereby reducing patient discomfort. To achieve this objective, we propose a vision-based deep reinforcement learning framework guided by monocular depth estimation. A realistic intestinal simulation environment was constructed in \textit{NVIDIA Omniverse} to train and evaluate autonomous navigation strategies. Furthermore, thousands of synthetic intraluminal images were generated using NVIDIA Replicator to fine-tune the Depth Anything model, enabling dense three-dimensional perception of the intestinal environment with a single monocular camera. Subsequently, we introduce a geometry-aware reward and penalty mechanism to enable accurate lumen tracking. Compared with the original Depth Anything model, our method improves $δ_{1}$ depth accuracy by 39.2% and reduces the navigation J-index by 0.67 relative to the second-best method, demonstrating the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed approach.
tldr: This paper presents a reinforcement learning framework for autonomous endoscopic navigation using synthetic data and a modified Depth Anything model, achieving improved depth accuracy and navigation performance in a simulated intestinal environment.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种基于强化学习的自主内窥镜导航框架，利用合成数据和改进的 Depth Anything 模型，在模拟肠道环境中实现了更高的深度准确度和导航性能。
summary_zh: 自主导航对于医疗和工业内窥镜机器人至关重要，它能够在狭窄的管状环境中实现安全高效的探索，而无需持续的人工干预。避免与内壁接触一直是先前方法面临的长期挑战。我们提出了一种基于柔性连续体结构的“跟随者”式内窥镜机器人，旨在最大程度地减少内窥镜主体与肠壁之间的接触，从而减轻患者的不适。为了实现这一目标，我们提出了一种基于视觉的深度强化学习框架，该框架由单目深度估计引导。在\textit{NVIDIA Omniverse}中构建了一个逼真的肠道模拟环境，用于训练和评估自主导航策略。此外，使用 NVIDIA Replicator 生成了数千张合成管腔内图像，用于微调 Depth Anything 模型，从而能够通过单个单目相机实现对肠道环境的密集三维感知。随后，我们引入了一种几何感知奖励和惩罚机制，以实现精确的管腔跟踪。与原始 Depth Anything 模型相比，我们的方法将 $δ_{1}$ 深度精度提高了 39.2%，并且相对于第二好的方法，导航 J 指数降低了 0.67，证明了所提出方法的鲁棒性和有效性。

title: Closing the Reality Gap: Zero-Shot Sim-to-Real Deployment for Dexterous Force-Based Grasping and Manipulation
summary: Human-like dexterous hands with multiple fingers offer human-level manipulation capabilities, but training control policies that can directly deploy on real hardware remains difficult due to contact-rich physics and imperfect actuation. We close this gap with a practical sim-to-real reinforcement learning (RL) framework that utilizes dense tactile feedback combined with joint torque sensing to explicitly regulate physical interactions. To enable effective sim-to-real transfer, we introduce (i) a computationally fast tactile simulation that computes distances between dense virtual tactile units and the object via parallel forward kinematics, providing high-rate, high-resolution touch signals needed by RL; (ii) a current-to-torque calibration that eliminates the need for torque sensors on dexterous hands by mapping motor current to joint torque; and (iii) actuator dynamics modeling to bridge the actuation gaps with randomization of non-ideal effects such as backlash, torque-speed saturation. Using an asymmetric actor-critic PPO pipeline trained entirely in simulation, our policies deploy directly to a five-finger hand. The resulting policies demonstrated two essential skills: (1) command-based, controllable grasp force tracking, and (2) reorientation of objects in the hand, both of which were robustly executed without fine-tuning on the robot. By combining tactile and torque in the observation space with effective sensing/actuation modeling, our system provides a practical solution to achieve reliable dexterous manipulation. To our knowledge, this is the first demonstration of controllable grasping on a multi-finger dexterous hand trained entirely in simulation and transferred zero-shot on real hardware.
tldr: This paper presents a sim-to-real reinforcement learning framework for dexterous hand control using tactile and torque feedback, achieving zero-shot transfer to a real robotic hand for grasping and object reorientation tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种基于触觉和扭矩反馈的灵巧手控制的模拟到真实强化学习框架，实现了零样本迁移到真实机器人手中的抓取和物体重新定向任务。
summary_zh: 具备多根手指的类人灵巧手提供了人类水平的操控能力，但由于富含接触的物理特性和不完善的驱动，训练可以直接部署在真实硬件上的控制策略仍然困难。我们通过一种实用的模拟到真实强化学习 (RL) 框架弥合了这一差距，该框架利用密集触觉反馈结合关节力矩感应来显式地调节物理交互。为了实现有效的模拟到真实迁移，我们引入了 (i) 一种计算快速的触觉模拟，通过并行正向运动学计算密集虚拟触觉单元与物体之间的距离，从而提供 RL 所需的高速率、高分辨率触觉信号；(ii) 电流到力矩的校准，通过将电机电流映射到关节力矩，消除了对灵巧手力矩传感器的需求；以及 (iii) 驱动器动力学建模，通过对反冲、转矩-速度饱和等非理想效果进行随机化，来弥合驱动方面的差距。使用完全在模拟中训练的非对称 actor-critic PPO 流程，我们的策略可以直接部署到五指手上。生成的策略展示了两个关键技能：（1）基于指令的可控抓取力跟踪，以及（2）手中物体的重新定向，两者都在没有在机器人上进行微调的情况下鲁棒地执行。通过将触觉和力矩结合在观测空间中，并配合有效的传感/驱动建模，我们的系统提供了一种实现可靠灵巧操作的实用解决方案。据我们所知，这是首次完全在模拟中训练并在真实硬件上零样本迁移的多指灵巧手上的可控抓取演示。

title: UltraLogic: Enhancing LLM Reasoning through Large-Scale Data Synthesis and Bipolar Float Reward
summary: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated significant potential in natural language processing , complex general-purpose reasoning requiring multi-step logic, planning, and verification remains a critical bottleneck. Although Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has succeeded in specific domains , the field lacks large-scale, high-quality, and difficulty-calibrated data for general reasoning. To address this, we propose UltraLogic, a framework that decouples the logical core of a problem from its natural language expression through a Code-based Solving methodology to automate high-quality data production. The framework comprises hundreds of unique task types and an automated calibration pipeline across ten difficulty levels. Furthermore, to mitigate binary reward sparsity and the Non-negative Reward Trap, we introduce the Bipolar Float Reward (BFR) mechanism, utilizing graded penalties to effectively distinguish perfect responses from those with logical flaws. Our experiments demonstrate that task diversity is the primary driver for reasoning enhancement , and that BFR, combined with a difficulty matching strategy, significantly improves training efficiency, guiding models toward global logical optima.
tldr: The paper introduces UltraLogic, a framework for enhancing LLM reasoning through large-scale data synthesis and a novel Bipolar Float Reward (BFR) mechanism, demonstrating improved reasoning and training efficiency.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了UltraLogic，一个通过大规模数据合成和新型双极浮点奖励（BFR）机制来增强LLM推理的框架，展示了改进的推理和训练效率。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型(LLM)在自然语言处理领域已展现出巨大潜力，但需要多步逻辑、规划和验证的复杂通用推理仍然是一个关键瓶颈。虽然基于可验证奖励的强化学习(RLVR)在特定领域取得了成功，但该领域缺乏大规模、高质量且难度校准的通用推理数据。为解决这个问题，我们提出了UltraLogic，一个通过基于代码的求解方法将问题的逻辑核心与其自然语言表达解耦的框架，从而自动化高质量数据的生成。该框架包含数百种独特的任务类型和一个跨越十个难度级别的自动化校准流水线。此外，为了缓解二元奖励的稀疏性和非负奖励陷阱，我们引入了双极浮点奖励(BFR)机制，利用分级惩罚来有效区分完美答案和存在逻辑缺陷的答案。我们的实验表明，任务多样性是推理能力增强的主要驱动力，并且BFR与难度匹配策略相结合可以显著提高训练效率，引导模型逼近全局逻辑最优解。

title: IBISAgent: Reinforcing Pixel-Level Visual Reasoning in MLLMs for Universal Biomedical Object Referring and Segmentation
summary: Recent research on medical MLLMs has gradually shifted its focus from image-level understanding to fine-grained, pixel-level comprehension. Although segmentation serves as the foundation for pixel-level understanding, existing approaches face two major challenges. First, they introduce implicit segmentation tokens and require simultaneous fine-tuning of both the MLLM and external pixel decoders, which increases the risk of catastrophic forgetting and limits generalization to out-of-domain scenarios. Second, most methods rely on single-pass reasoning and lack the capability to iteratively refine segmentation results, leading to suboptimal performance. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel agentic MLLM, named IBISAgent, that reformulates segmentation as a vision-centric, multi-step decision-making process. IBISAgent enables MLLMs to generate interleaved reasoning and text-based click actions, invoke segmentation tools, and produce high-quality masks without architectural modifications. By iteratively performing multi-step visual reasoning on masked image features, IBISAgent naturally supports mask refinement and promotes the development of pixel-level visual reasoning capabilities. We further design a two-stage training framework consisting of cold-start supervised fine-tuning and agentic reinforcement learning with tailored, fine-grained rewards, enhancing the model's robustness in complex medical referring and reasoning segmentation tasks. Extensive experiments demonstrate that IBISAgent consistently outperforms both closed-source and open-source SOTA methods. All datasets, code, and trained models will be released publicly.
tldr: The paper introduces IBISAgent, an agentic MLLM that uses reinforcement learning and iterative visual reasoning to improve pixel-level biomedical object referring and segmentation without architectural modifications to the MLLM.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为IBISAgent的智能MLLM，它使用强化学习和迭代视觉推理来改进像素级别的生物医学对象指代和分割，而无需对MLLM进行结构修改。
summary_zh: 对医学多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）的最新研究已逐渐将其重点从图像级理解转向精细的像素级理解。尽管分割是像素级理解的基础，但现有方法面临两个主要挑战。首先，它们引入隐式分割token并需要同时微调MLLM和外部像素解码器，这增加了灾难性遗忘的风险，并限制了对领域外场景的泛化能力。其次，大多数方法依赖于单次推理，缺乏迭代优化分割结果的能力，导致性能欠佳。为了克服这些限制，我们提出了一种新型的代理式MLLM，名为IBISAgent，它将分割重新定义为以视觉为中心的、多步骤的决策过程。IBISAgent使MLLM能够生成交错的推理和基于文本的点击动作，调用分割工具，并生成高质量的掩码，而无需架构修改。通过在掩码图像特征上迭代执行多步骤视觉推理，IBISAgent自然地支持掩码优化，并促进像素级视觉推理能力的开发。我们进一步设计了一个两阶段训练框架，包括冷启动监督微调和具有定制的、细粒度奖励的代理式强化学习，增强了模型在复杂的医学指代和推理分割任务中的鲁棒性。大量实验表明，IBISAgent始终优于闭源和开源的最先进（SOTA）方法。所有数据集、代码和训练好的模型都将公开发布。

title: Aligning Findings with Diagnosis: A Self-Consistent Reinforcement Learning Framework for Trustworthy Radiology Reporting
summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have shown strong potential for radiology report generation, yet their clinical translation is hindered by architectural heterogeneity and the prevalence of factual hallucinations. Standard supervised fine-tuning often fails to strictly align linguistic outputs with visual evidence, while existing reinforcement learning approaches struggle with either prohibitive computational costs or limited exploration. To address these challenges, we propose a comprehensive framework for self-consistent radiology report generation. First, we conduct a systematic evaluation to identify optimal vision encoder and LLM backbone configurations for medical imaging. Building on this foundation, we introduce a novel "Reason-then-Summarize" architecture optimized via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO). This framework restructures generation into two distinct components: a think block for detailed findings and an answer block for structured disease labels. By utilizing a multi-dimensional composite reward function, we explicitly penalize logical discrepancies between the generated narrative and the final diagnosis. Extensive experiments on the MIMIC-CXR benchmark demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in clinical efficacy metrics and significantly reduces hallucinations compared to strong supervised baselines.
tldr: This paper introduces a self-consistent reinforcement learning framework (Reason-then-Summarize + GRPO) for radiology report generation, aiming to improve alignment between visual evidence and text while reducing factual hallucinations in MLLMs.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一个自洽的强化学习框架（Reason-then-Summarize + GRPO）用于生成放射学报告，旨在提高视觉证据和文本之间的一致性，同时减少 MLLM 中的事实幻觉。
summary_zh: 多模态大型语言模型（MLLM）在放射科报告生成方面展现出强大的潜力，但它们在临床转化方面受到架构异构性和事实性幻觉普遍存在的阻碍。标准的监督式微调常常无法严格地将语言输出与视觉证据对齐，而现有的强化学习方法则面临着计算成本过高或探索能力有限的困境。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了一个用于自洽放射科报告生成的综合框架。首先，我们进行系统的评估，以确定用于医学影像的最佳视觉编码器和LLM主干配置。在此基础上，我们引入了一种新颖的“推理-然后-总结”架构，并通过组相对策略优化（GRPO）进行优化。该框架将生成过程重构为两个不同的组成部分：用于详细发现的“思考”块和用于结构化疾病标签的“回答”块。通过采用多维复合奖励函数，我们明确地惩罚生成叙述与最终诊断之间的逻辑差异。在MIMIC-CXR基准上的大量实验表明，与强大的监督基线相比，我们的方法在临床疗效指标上取得了最先进的性能，并显著减少了幻觉。

title: Ratio-Variance Regularized Policy Optimization for Efficient LLM Fine-tuning
summary: On-policy reinforcement learning (RL), particularly Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) and Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), has become the dominant paradigm for fine-tuning large language models (LLMs). While policy ratio clipping stabilizes training, this heuristic hard constraint incurs a fundamental cost: it indiscriminately truncates gradients from high-return yet high-divergence actions, suppressing rare but highly informative "eureka moments" in complex reasoning. Moreover, once data becomes slightly stale, hard clipping renders it unusable, leading to severe sample inefficiency. In this work, we revisit the trust-region objective in policy optimization and show that explicitly constraining the \emph{variance (second central moment) of the policy ratio} provides a principled and smooth relaxation of hard clipping. This distributional constraint stabilizes policy updates while preserving gradient signals from valuable trajectories. Building on this insight, we propose $R^2VPO$ (Ratio-Variance Regularized Policy Optimization), a novel primal-dual framework that supports stable on-policy learning and enables principled off-policy data reuse by dynamically reweighting stale samples rather than discarding them. We extensively evaluate $R^2VPO$ on fine-tuning state-of-the-art LLMs, including DeepSeek-Distill-Qwen-1.5B and the openPangu-Embedded series (1B and 7B), across challenging mathematical reasoning benchmarks. Experimental results show that $R^2VPO$ consistently achieves superior asymptotic performance, with average relative gains of up to 17% over strong clipping-based baselines, while requiring approximately 50% fewer rollouts to reach convergence. These findings establish ratio-variance control as a promising direction for improving both stability and data efficiency in RL-based LLM alignment.
tldr: The paper introduces R^2VPO, a novel RL method for LLM fine-tuning that replaces hard policy ratio clipping with a variance constraint, improving stability, data efficiency, and performance on mathematical reasoning tasks by up to 17% compared to clipping-based baselines using 50% fewer rollouts.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了R^2VPO，一种用于LLM微调的新型RL方法，它用方差约束取代了策略比率的硬裁剪，提高了稳定性和数据效率，并在数学推理任务中的性能比基于裁剪的基线提高了17%，且使用少了50%的rollouts。
summary_zh: 在微调大型语言模型(LLM)方面，在策略强化学习(RL)，特别是近端策略优化(PPO)和组相对策略优化(GRPO)，已成为主导范式。虽然策略比率裁剪能够稳定训练，但这种启发式的硬约束带来一个根本性的代价：它不加区分地截断了来自高回报但高差异动作的梯度，抑制了复杂推理中稀有但极具信息量的“尤里卡时刻”。此外，一旦数据稍微过时，硬裁剪会使其无法使用，导致严重的样本效率低下。在这项工作中，我们重新审视策略优化中的置信域目标，并表明显式地约束\emph{策略比率的方差（二阶中心矩）}提供了对硬裁剪的一种原则性的平滑松弛。这种分布约束稳定了策略更新，同时保留了来自有价值轨迹的梯度信号。基于此洞见，我们提出了$R^2VPO$（比率-方差正则化策略优化），一种新颖的原对偶框架，支持稳定的在策略学习，并通过动态地重新加权过时样本而不是丢弃它们，来实现原则性的离策略数据重用。我们广泛地评估了$R^2VPO$在微调最先进的LLMs上的效果，包括DeepSeek-Distill-Qwen-1.5B和openPangu-Embedded系列（1B和7B），涵盖了具有挑战性的数学推理基准。实验结果表明，$R^2VPO$始终能够实现卓越的渐近性能，与强大的基于裁剪的基线相比，平均相对增益高达17%，同时达到收敛所需的迭代次数减少了约50%。这些发现确立了比率-方差控制作为改善基于RL的LLM对齐的稳定性和数据效率的一个有前景的方向。

title: In-Context Reinforcement Learning through Bayesian Fusion of Context and Value Prior
summary: In-context reinforcement learning (ICRL) promises fast adaptation to unseen environments without parameter updates, but current methods either cannot improve beyond the training distribution or require near-optimal data, limiting practical adoption. We introduce SPICE, a Bayesian ICRL method that learns a prior over Q-values via deep ensemble and updates this prior at test-time using in-context information through Bayesian updates. To recover from poor priors resulting from training on sub-optimal data, our online inference follows an Upper-Confidence Bound rule that favours exploration and adaptation. We prove that SPICE achieves regret-optimal behaviour in both stochastic bandits and finite-horizon MDPs, even when pretrained only on suboptimal trajectories. We validate these findings empirically across bandit and control benchmarks. SPICE achieves near-optimal decisions on unseen tasks, substantially reduces regret compared to prior ICRL and meta-RL approaches while rapidly adapting to unseen tasks and remaining robust under distribution shift.
tldr: The paper introduces SPICE, a Bayesian In-Context Reinforcement Learning (ICRL) method that leverages deep ensembles and Bayesian updates for rapid adaptation to unseen environments, even with suboptimal training data, achieving regret-optimal behavior.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种贝叶斯上下文强化学习（ICRL）方法SPICE，它利用深度集成和贝叶斯更新来实现对未见环境的快速适应，即使使用次优训练数据也能达到遗憾最优行为。
summary_zh: 上下文强化学习 (ICRL) 有望在无需参数更新的情况下快速适应未见环境，但目前的方法要么无法超越训练分布，要么需要接近最优的数据，限制了其实际应用。我们提出了 SPICE，一种贝叶斯 ICRL 方法，它通过深度集成学习一个关于 Q 值的先验分布，并在测试时利用上下文信息通过贝叶斯更新来更新这个先验分布。为了从基于次优数据训练得到的较差先验分布中恢复，我们的在线推断遵循一个上置信界准则，该准则偏向于探索和适应。我们证明了 SPICE 在随机老虎机和有限视界 MDP 中均可实现遗憾最优行为，即使仅在次优轨迹上进行预训练。我们在老虎机和控制基准测试中验证了这些发现。SPICE 在未见任务上实现了接近最优的决策，与之前的 ICRL 和元强化学习方法相比，显著降低了遗憾值，同时迅速适应未见任务并在分布偏移下保持鲁棒性。

title: TA-Prompting: Enhancing Video Large Language Models for Dense Video Captioning via Temporal Anchors
summary: Dense video captioning aims to interpret and describe all temporally localized events throughout an input video. Recent state-of-the-art methods leverage large language models (LLMs) to provide detailed moment descriptions for video data. However, existing VideoLLMs remain challenging in identifying precise event boundaries in untrimmed videos, causing the generated captions to be not properly grounded. In this paper, we propose TA-Prompting, which enhances VideoLLMs via Temporal Anchors that learn to precisely localize events and prompt the VideoLLMs to perform temporal-aware video event understanding. During inference, in order to properly determine the output caption sequence from an arbitrary number of events presented within a video, we introduce an event coherent sampling strategy to select event captions with sufficient coherence across temporal events and cross-modal similarity with the given video. Through extensive experiments on benchmark datasets, we show that our TA-Prompting is favorable against state-of-the-art VideoLLMs, yielding superior performance on dense video captioning and temporal understanding tasks including moment retrieval and temporalQA.
tldr: The paper introduces TA-Prompting, a method to improve VideoLLMs for dense video captioning using temporal anchors for precise event localization and an event-coherent sampling strategy for generating consistent captions.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了TA-Prompting，一种利用时间锚点精确定位事件并通过事件一致性采样策略生成一致字幕的方法，从而改进用于密集视频字幕的VideoLLM。
summary_zh: 稠密视频描述旨在解读和描述输入视频中所有时间局部化的事件。 近期最先进的方法利用大型语言模型（LLM）为视频数据提供详细的时刻描述。 然而，现有的视频LLM在识别未剪辑视频中精确的事件边界方面仍然面临挑战，导致生成的描述没有得到适当的对齐。 在本文中，我们提出了TA- Prompting，通过时序锚点增强视频LLM，这些时序锚点学习精确定位事件并提示视频LLM执行时序感知的视频事件理解。 在推理过程中，为了从视频中呈现的任意数量的事件中正确地确定输出描述序列，我们引入了一种事件连贯性采样策略，以选择具有足够的跨时间事件连贯性以及与给定视频的跨模态相似性的事件描述。 通过在基准数据集上的大量实验，我们表明我们的TA-Prompting优于最先进的视频LLM，并在稠密视频描述和时间理解任务（包括时刻检索和时间问答）上产生了卓越的性能。

title: VLM4VLA: Revisiting Vision-Language-Models in Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, which integrate pretrained large Vision-Language Models (VLM) into their policy backbone, are gaining significant attention for their promising generalization capabilities. This paper revisits a fundamental yet seldom systematically studied question: how VLM choice and competence translate to downstream VLA policies performance? We introduce VLM4VLA, a minimal adaptation pipeline that converts general-purpose VLMs into VLA policies using only a small set of new learnable parameters for fair and efficient comparison. Despite its simplicity, VLM4VLA proves surprisingly competitive with more sophisticated network designs. Through extensive empirical studies on various downstream tasks across three benchmarks, we find that while VLM initialization offers a consistent benefit over training from scratch, a VLM's general capabilities are poor predictors of its downstream task performance. This challenges common assumptions, indicating that standard VLM competence is necessary but insufficient for effective embodied control. We further investigate the impact of specific embodied capabilities by fine-tuning VLMs on seven auxiliary embodied tasks (e.g., embodied QA, visual pointing, depth estimation). Contrary to intuition, improving a VLM's performance on specific embodied skills does not guarantee better downstream control performance. Finally, modality-level ablations identify the visual module in VLM, rather than the language component, as the primary performance bottleneck. We demonstrate that injecting control-relevant supervision into the vision encoder of the VLM yields consistent gains, even when the encoder remains frozen during downstream fine-tuning. This isolates a persistent domain gap between current VLM pretraining objectives and the requirements of embodied action-planning.
tldr: This paper investigates how the choice of VLMs affects VLA performance, finding that general VLM capabilities don't directly translate to better embodied control and that the visual module is a key bottleneck. They also demonstrate that targeted supervision of the visual encoder boosts performance.
tldr_zh: 该论文研究了VLM的选择如何影响VLA性能，发现通用VLM的能力并不能直接转化为更好的具身控制，并且视觉模块是关键瓶颈。他们还证明，对视觉编码器进行有针对性的监督可以提高性能。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型因其有前景的泛化能力而备受关注，这些模型将预训练的大型视觉-语言模型 (VLM) 集成到其策略主干中。本文重新审视了一个基本但很少被系统研究的问题：VLM 的选择和能力如何转化为下游 VLA 策略的性能？我们提出了 VLM4VLA，这是一个最小化适配流程，它仅使用一小部分新的可学习参数将通用 VLM 转换为 VLA 策略，以进行公平和高效的比较。尽管 VLM4VLA 非常简单，但它被证明与更复杂的网络设计相比具有出人意料的竞争力。通过对三个基准测试中的各种下游任务进行广泛的实证研究，我们发现，虽然 VLM 初始化提供了优于从头开始训练的一致优势，但 VLM 的一般能力对其下游任务性能的预测效果不佳。这挑战了常见的假设，即标准的 VLM 能力对于有效的具身控制是必要的但不足够的。我们进一步研究了特定具身能力的影响，方法是在七个辅助具身任务（例如，具身问答、视觉指向、深度估计）上微调 VLM。与直觉相反，提高 VLM 在特定具身技能上的表现并不能保证更好的下游控制性能。最后，模态级别的消融实验表明，VLM 中的视觉模块，而不是语言组件，是主要的性能瓶颈。我们证明了将与控制相关的监督信息注入到 VLM 的视觉编码器中可以产生持续的收益，即使该编码器在下游微调期间保持冻结状态也是如此。这突显了当前 VLM 预训练目标与具身行动规划要求之间存在持续的领域差距。

title: Sample-Efficient Neurosymbolic Deep Reinforcement Learning
summary: Reinforcement Learning (RL) is a well-established framework for sequential decision-making in complex environments. However, state-of-the-art Deep RL (DRL) algorithms typically require large training datasets and often struggle to generalize beyond small-scale training scenarios, even within standard benchmarks. We propose a neuro-symbolic DRL approach that integrates background symbolic knowledge to improve sample efficiency and generalization to more challenging, unseen tasks. Partial policies defined for simple domain instances, where high performance is easily attained, are transferred as useful priors to accelerate learning in more complex settings and avoid tuning DRL parameters from scratch. To do so, partial policies are represented as logical rules, and online reasoning is performed to guide the training process through two mechanisms: (i) biasing the action distribution during exploration, and (ii) rescaling Q-values during exploitation. This neuro-symbolic integration enhances interpretability and trustworthiness while accelerating convergence, particularly in sparse-reward environments and tasks with long planning horizons. We empirically validate our methodology on challenging variants of gridworld environments, both in the fully observable and partially observable setting. We show improved performance over a state-of-the-art reward machine baseline.
tldr: This paper introduces a neuro-symbolic Deep RL approach that leverages symbolic knowledge to improve sample efficiency and generalization in challenging RL environments by transferring partial policies as logical rules.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种神经符号深度强化学习方法，该方法利用符号知识通过将部分策略作为逻辑规则转移来提高具有挑战性的强化学习环境中的样本效率和泛化能力。
summary_zh: 强化学习 (RL) 是一种在复杂环境中进行序贯决策的成熟框架。然而，最先进的深度强化学习 (DRL) 算法通常需要大量的训练数据集，并且即使在标准基准测试中，也往往难以泛化到小规模训练场景之外。我们提出了一种神经符号深度强化学习方法，该方法集成了背景符号知识，以提高样本效率和泛化能力，从而解决更具挑战性的、未见过的任务。为简单领域实例（其中易于获得高性能）定义的部分策略被转移为有用的先验知识，以加速在更复杂环境中的学习，并避免从头开始调整 DRL 参数。为此，部分策略被表示为逻辑规则，并执行在线推理，通过两种机制指导训练过程：（i）在探索期间偏置动作分布，以及（ii）在利用期间重新缩放 Q 值。这种神经符号集成增强了解释性和可信赖性，同时加速了收敛，尤其是在稀疏奖励环境和具有长规划范围的任务中。我们在具有挑战性的网格世界环境变体中，包括完全可观察和部分可观察的设置，对我们的方法进行了实证验证。结果表明，与最先进的奖励机器基线相比，性能有所提高。

title: LLM Agent Framework for Intelligent Change Analysis in Urban Environment using Remote Sensing Imagery
summary: Existing change detection methods often lack the versatility to handle diverse real-world queries and the intelligence for comprehensive analysis. This paper presents a general agent framework, integrating Large Language Models (LLM) with vision foundation models to form ChangeGPT. A hierarchical structure is employed to mitigate hallucination. The agent was evaluated on a curated dataset of 140 questions categorized by real-world scenarios, encompassing various question types (e.g., Size, Class, Number) and complexities. The evaluation assessed the agent's tool selection ability (Precision/Recall) and overall query accuracy (Match). ChangeGPT, especially with a GPT-4-turbo backend, demonstrated superior performance, achieving a 90.71 % Match rate. Its strength lies particularly in handling change-related queries requiring multi-step reasoning and robust tool selection. Practical effectiveness was further validated through a real-world urban change monitoring case study in Qianhai Bay, Shenzhen. By providing intelligence, adaptability, and multi-type change analysis, ChangeGPT offers a powerful solution for decision-making in remote sensing applications.
tldr: The paper presents ChangeGPT, an LLM-based agent framework leveraging vision foundation models for intelligent change analysis in urban environments using remote sensing imagery, achieving high accuracy in answering complex, multi-step reasoning queries related to change detection.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了ChangeGPT，一个基于LLM的智能体框架，利用视觉基础模型对城市环境中的遥感图像进行智能变更分析。该框架在回答与变更检测相关的复杂、多步骤推理查询方面表现出很高的准确性。
summary_zh: 现有变化检测方法通常缺乏处理多样化真实世界查询的通用性，以及进行全面分析的智能性。本文提出一种通用代理框架，将大型语言模型（LLM）与视觉基础模型相结合，形成ChangeGPT。采用分层结构来缓解幻觉问题。该代理在一个包含140个问题的精选数据集上进行了评估，这些问题按真实世界场景分类，涵盖了各种问题类型（例如，大小、类别、数量）和复杂性。 评估考察了代理的工具选择能力（精确率/召回率）和整体查询准确率（匹配率）。ChangeGPT，尤其是采用GPT-4-turbo后端的ChangeGPT，表现出卓越的性能，实现了90.71%的匹配率。其优势尤其在于处理需要多步骤推理和稳健工具选择的与变化相关的查询。通过深圳前海湾的真实城市变化监测案例研究，进一步验证了其在实践中的有效性。通过提供智能、适应性和多类型变化分析，ChangeGPT为遥感应用中的决策提供了强大的解决方案。

title: Prioritized Replay for RL Post-training
summary: We introduce a problem-level prioritization framework for RL post-training of large language models. Building on insights from prioritized replay in deep RL, as well as prior observations that rollouts with intermediate success rates tend to produce stronger learning signals under methods such as GRPO, our approach selects problems according to a simple, model-driven priority score derived from empirical success statistics. In contrast to conventional curriculum strategies that emphasize easier tasks early in training, the resulting schedule naturally focuses training on problems that are neither consistently solved nor consistently failed, while deprioritizing those that contribute little gradient information. The method yields a continuously adapting and automatic prioritization process that requires no predefined difficulty tiers, auxiliary predictors, or external labels. We further introduce lightweight mechanisms for practical deployment, including heap-based prioritized sampling and periodic retesting of solved and unsolved problems to mitigate starvation and forgetting. Overall, the approach offers a principled and scalable alternative to manually designed curricula while aligning data selection directly with the dynamics of GRPO-based post-training.
tldr: The paper introduces a problem-level prioritization framework for RL post-training of large language models that automatically focuses training on problems with intermediate success rates using a model-driven priority score.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种用于大型语言模型RL训练后阶段的问题级别优先级框架，该框架使用模型驱动的优先级分数自动将训练重点放在具有中等成功率的问题上。
summary_zh: 我们引入了一种针对大型语言模型强化学习后训练的问题级别优先级框架。该框架建立在深度强化学习中的优先级回放以及先前有关中间成功率的轨迹倾向于在GRPO等方法下产生更强学习信号的观察之上，我们的方法根据从经验成功统计数据中推导出的一个简单的、模型驱动的优先级分数来选择问题。与强调在训练早期进行更容易任务的传统课程策略相反，由此产生的训练计划自然地将训练重点放在既不总是成功解决，也不总是失败的问题上，同时降低那些贡献较少梯度信息的问题的优先级。该方法产生一个持续适应和自动化的优先级排序过程，该过程不需要预定义的难度等级、辅助预测器或外部标签。我们进一步引入了用于实际部署的轻量级机制，包括基于堆的优先级采样以及对已解决和未解决问题的定期重新测试，以减轻“饥饿”和遗忘。总的来说，该方法提供了一种有原则且可扩展的替代手动设计的课程的方法，同时直接将数据选择与基于GRPO的后训练的动态相一致。

title: SimpleMem: Efficient Lifelong Memory for LLM Agents
summary: To support reliable long-term interaction in complex environments, LLM agents require memory systems that efficiently manage historical experiences. Existing approaches either retain full interaction histories via passive context extension, leading to substantial redundancy, or rely on iterative reasoning to filter noise, incurring high token costs. To address this challenge, we introduce SimpleMem, an efficient memory framework based on semantic lossless compression. We propose a three-stage pipeline designed to maximize information density and token utilization: (1) \textit{Semantic Structured Compression}, which applies entropy-aware filtering to distill unstructured interactions into compact, multi-view indexed memory units; (2) \textit{Recursive Memory Consolidation}, an asynchronous process that integrates related units into higher-level abstract representations to reduce redundancy; and (3) \textit{Adaptive Query-Aware Retrieval}, which dynamically adjusts retrieval scope based on query complexity to construct precise context efficiently. Experiments on benchmark datasets show that our method consistently outperforms baseline approaches in accuracy, retrieval efficiency, and inference cost, achieving an average F1 improvement of 26.4% while reducing inference-time token consumption by up to 30-fold, demonstrating a superior balance between performance and efficiency. Code is available at https://github.com/aiming-lab/SimpleMem.
tldr: SimpleMem is proposed as an efficient memory framework for LLM agents, using semantic lossless compression to improve accuracy and reduce token consumption.
tldr_zh: SimpleMem被提出作为LLM Agent的高效记忆框架，它使用语义无损压缩来提高准确性并减少token消耗。
summary_zh: 为了支持在复杂环境中可靠的长期交互，LLM智能体需要能够高效管理历史经验的记忆系统。现有的方法要么通过被动上下文扩展保留完整的交互历史，导致大量的冗余，要么依赖于迭代推理来过滤噪声，产生高昂的token成本。为了应对这一挑战，我们引入了SimpleMem，一个基于语义无损压缩的高效记忆框架。我们提出了一个三阶段的流程，旨在最大化信息密度和token利用率：（1）\textit{语义结构化压缩}，应用熵感知过滤将非结构化交互提炼成紧凑的、多视图索引的记忆单元；（2）\textit{递归记忆整合}，一个异步过程，将相关的单元整合到更高层次的抽象表示中，以减少冗余；（3）\textit{自适应查询感知检索}，根据查询的复杂性动态调整检索范围，从而高效地构建精确的上下文。在基准数据集上的实验表明，我们的方法在准确性、检索效率和推理成本方面始终优于基线方法，实现了平均F1提升26.4%，同时降低了高达30倍的推理时token消耗，展示了性能和效率之间的卓越平衡。代码可在https://github.com/aiming-lab/SimpleMem获取。

title: ReCCur: A Recursive Corner-Case Curation Framework for Robust Vision-Language Understanding in Open and Edge Scenarios
summary: Corner cases are rare or extreme scenarios that drive real-world failures, but they are difficult to curate at scale: web data are noisy, labels are brittle, and edge deployments preclude large retraining. We present ReCCur (Recursive Corner-Case Curation), a low-compute framework that converts noisy web imagery into auditable fine-grained labels via a multi-agent recursive pipeline. First, large-scale data acquisition and filtering expands a domain vocabulary with a vision-language model (VLM), crawls the web, and enforces tri-modal (image, description, keyword) consistency with light human spot checks to yield refined candidates. Next, mixture-of-experts knowledge distillation uses complementary encoders (e.g., CLIP, DINOv2, BEiT) for kNN voting with dual-confidence activation and uncertainty sampling, converging to a high-precision set. Finally, region-evidence VLM adversarial labeling pairs a proposer (multi-granularity regions and semantic cues) with a validator (global and local chained consistency) to produce explainable labels and close the loop. On realistic corner-case scenarios (e.g., flooded-car inspection), ReCCur runs on consumer-grade GPUs, steadily improves purity and separability, and requires minimal human supervision, providing a practical substrate for downstream training and evaluation under resource constraints. Code and dataset will be released.
tldr: The paper introduces ReCCur, a framework for curating corner-case data for vision-language models using a recursive pipeline with web imagery, knowledge distillation, and adversarial labeling, designed for resource-constrained environments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为ReCCur的框架，用于创建视觉-语言模型的极端案例数据，该框架利用递归流水线，包括网络图像数据，知识蒸馏和对抗标签，专为资源有限的环境设计。
summary_zh: 拐角案例是驱动现实世界故障的罕见或极端场景，但难以大规模管理：网络数据嘈杂，标签脆弱，边缘部署阻止了大规模的重新训练。我们提出了一种低计算框架 ReCCur（递归拐角案例管理），它通过多代理递归管道将嘈杂的网络图像转换为可审计的细粒度标签。首先，大规模的数据采集和过滤利用视觉-语言模型（VLM）扩展了领域词汇表，并爬取网络，通过少量人工抽查来强制执行三模态（图像、描述、关键词）一致性，从而产生精炼的候选集。其次，混合专家知识蒸馏采用互补的编码器（例如，CLIP、DINOv2、BEiT）进行 kNN 投票，并结合双置信度激活和不确定性采样，最终收敛到一组高精度的数据集。最后，区域证据 VLM 对抗性标注将提议器（多粒度区域和语义线索）与验证器（全局和局部链式一致性）配对，从而产生可解释的标签并闭合循环。在真实的拐角案例场景中（例如，被淹汽车的检查），ReCCur 可以在消费级 GPU 上运行，稳步提高纯度和可分离性，并且只需要极少的人工监督，从而为资源约束下的下游训练和评估提供了一个实用的基础。代码和数据集将会发布。

title: SketchThinker-R1: Towards Efficient Sketch-Style Reasoning in Large Multimodal Models
summary: Despite the empirical success of extensive, step-by-step reasoning in large multimodal models, long reasoning processes inevitably incur substantial computational overhead, i.e., in terms of higher token costs and increased response time, which undermines inference efficiency. In contrast, humans often employ sketch-style reasoning: a concise, goal-directed cognitive process that prioritizes salient information and enables efficient problem-solving. Inspired by this cognitive efficiency, we propose SketchThinker-R1, which incentivizes sketch-style reasoning ability in large multimodal models. Our method consists of three primary stages. In the Sketch-Mode Cold Start stage, we convert standard long reasoning process into sketch-style reasoning and finetune base multimodal model, instilling initial sketch-style reasoning capability. Next, we train SketchJudge Reward Model, which explicitly evaluates thinking process of model and assigns higher scores to sketch-style reasoning. Finally, we conduct Sketch-Thinking Reinforcement Learning under supervision of SketchJudge to further generalize sketch-style reasoning ability. Experimental evaluation on four benchmarks reveals that our SketchThinker-R1 achieves over 64% reduction in reasoning token cost without compromising final answer accuracy. Qualitative analysis further shows that sketch-style reasoning focuses more on key cues during problem solving.
tldr: The paper introduces SketchThinker-R1, a method for encouraging efficient, sketch-style reasoning in large multimodal models, reducing token costs significantly without sacrificing accuracy, by using finetuning and reinforcement learning.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了SketchThinker-R1，一种通过微调和强化学习鼓励大型多模态模型进行高效的草图式推理的方法，显著降低了token成本，同时不牺牲准确性。
summary_zh: 大型多模态模型在进行广泛、逐步推理方面取得了实证上的成功，但漫长的推理过程不可避免地会导致巨大的计算开销，例如更高的Token成本和更长的响应时间，这会降低推理效率。相比之下，人类通常采用速写式推理：一种简洁、目标导向的认知过程，优先考虑显著信息并实现高效的问题解决。受这种认知效率的启发，我们提出了SketchThinker-R1，旨在激励大型多模态模型具备速写式推理能力。我们的方法包含三个主要阶段。在速写模式冷启动阶段，我们将标准的长推理过程转换为速写式推理，并对基础多模态模型进行微调，从而灌输初始的速写式推理能力。接下来，我们训练SketchJudge奖励模型，该模型显式地评估模型的思考过程，并为速写式推理分配更高的分数。最后，我们在SketchJudge的监督下进行速写式思考强化学习，以进一步泛化速写式推理能力。在四个基准数据集上的实验评估表明，我们的SketchThinker-R1在不影响最终答案准确性的前提下，实现了超过64%的推理Token成本降低。定性分析进一步表明，速写式推理在问题解决过程中更关注关键线索。

title: Talk2Move: Reinforcement Learning for Text-Instructed Object-Level Geometric Transformation in Scenes
summary: We introduce Talk2Move, a reinforcement learning (RL) based diffusion framework for text-instructed spatial transformation of objects within scenes. Spatially manipulating objects in a scene through natural language poses a challenge for multimodal generation systems. While existing text-based manipulation methods can adjust appearance or style, they struggle to perform object-level geometric transformations-such as translating, rotating, or resizing objects-due to scarce paired supervision and pixel-level optimization limits. Talk2Move employs Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to explore geometric actions through diverse rollouts generated from input images and lightweight textual variations, removing the need for costly paired data. A spatial reward guided model aligns geometric transformations with linguistic description, while off-policy step evaluation and active step sampling improve learning efficiency by focusing on informative transformation stages. Furthermore, we design object-centric spatial rewards that evaluate displacement, rotation, and scaling behaviors directly, enabling interpretable and coherent transformations. Experiments on curated benchmarks demonstrate that Talk2Move achieves precise, consistent, and semantically faithful object transformations, outperforming existing text-guided editing approaches in both spatial accuracy and scene coherence.
tldr: Talk2Move is a reinforcement learning framework that enables object-level geometric transformations within scenes based on natural language instructions, using Group Relative Policy Optimization and spatial reward functions for precise and coherent manipulation.
tldr_zh: Talk2Move是一个基于强化学习的框架，它能够根据自然语言指令在场景中进行物体级别的几何变换。该框架使用组相对策略优化和空间奖励函数来实现精确和连贯的物体操作。
summary_zh: 我们引入Talk2Move，一个基于强化学习（RL）的扩散框架，用于场景中物体的文本指令空间变换。通过自然语言空间操控场景中的物体对多模态生成系统提出了挑战。虽然现有的基于文本的操控方法可以调整外观或风格，但由于成对监督的稀缺性和像素级优化限制，它们难以执行物体级的几何变换——例如平移、旋转或缩放物体。Talk2Move采用群相对策略优化（GRPO）来探索几何动作，通过从输入图像和轻量级文本变体中生成的多样化rollout，从而无需昂贵的成对数据。空间奖励引导模型将几何变换与语言描述对齐，而离线策略步评估和主动步采样通过关注信息丰富的变换阶段来提高学习效率。此外，我们设计了以对象为中心的空间奖励，直接评估位移、旋转和缩放行为，从而实现可解释且连贯的变换。在精心策划的基准测试上的实验表明，Talk2Move实现了精确、一致和语义忠实的对象变换，在空间准确性和场景连贯性方面均优于现有的文本引导编辑方法。

title: Critic-Guided Reinforcement Unlearning in Text-to-Image Diffusion
summary: Machine unlearning in text-to-image diffusion models aims to remove targeted concepts while preserving overall utility. Prior diffusion unlearning methods typically rely on supervised weight edits or global penalties; reinforcement-learning (RL) approaches, while flexible, often optimize sparse end-of-trajectory rewards, yielding high-variance updates and weak credit assignment. We present a general RL framework for diffusion unlearning that treats denoising as a sequential decision process and introduces a timestep-aware critic with noisy-step rewards. Concretely, we train a CLIP-based reward predictor on noisy latents and use its per-step signal to compute advantage estimates for policy-gradient updates of the reverse diffusion kernel. Our algorithm is simple to implement, supports off-policy reuse, and plugs into standard text-to-image backbones. Across multiple concepts, the method achieves better or comparable forgetting to strong baselines while maintaining image quality and benign prompt fidelity; ablations show that (i) per-step critics and (ii) noisy-conditioned rewards are key to stability and effectiveness. We release code and evaluation scripts to facilitate reproducibility and future research on RL-based diffusion unlearning.
tldr: This paper introduces a reinforcement learning framework with a novel timestep-aware critic to improve machine unlearning in text-to-image diffusion models, demonstrating better or comparable forgetting with maintained image quality.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种新的强化学习框架，该框架具有时间步感知的评论家，以改进文本到图像扩散模型中的机器遗忘，证明其具有更好或可比的遗忘效果，同时保持图像质量。
summary_zh: 文本到图像扩散模型中的机器学习遗忘旨在移除目标概念，同时保持整体效用。先前的扩散遗忘方法通常依赖于有监督的权重编辑或全局惩罚；而强化学习（RL）方法虽然灵活，但通常优化稀疏的轨迹终点奖励，导致高方差的更新和微弱的信用分配。我们提出了一种用于扩散遗忘的通用 RL 框架，该框架将去噪视为序列决策过程，并引入具有噪声步奖励的timestep感知评论家。具体而言，我们在带噪声的潜在变量上训练一个基于 CLIP 的奖励预测器，并使用其每步信号来计算反向扩散核的策略梯度更新的优势估计。我们的算法易于实现，支持离线策略重用，并可插入到标准的文本到图像骨干网络中。在多个概念上，该方法实现了优于或者可与强基线相媲美的遗忘效果，同时保持了图像质量和良性提示的保真度；消融实验表明，（i）每步评论家和（ii）噪声条件奖励是稳定性和有效性的关键。我们发布了代码和评估脚本，以促进基于 RL 的扩散遗忘的可重复性和未来研究。

title: Empowering Reliable Visual-Centric Instruction Following in MLLMs
summary: Evaluating the instruction-following (IF) capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) is essential for rigorously assessing how faithfully model outputs adhere to user-specified intentions. Nevertheless, existing benchmarks for evaluating MLLMs' instruction-following capability primarily focus on verbal instructions in the textual modality. These limitations hinder a thorough analysis of instruction-following capabilities, as they overlook the implicit constraints embedded in the semantically rich visual modality. To address this gap, we introduce VC-IFEval, a new benchmark accompanied by a systematically constructed dataset that evaluates MLLMs' instruction-following ability under multimodal settings. Our benchmark systematically incorporates vision-dependent constraints into instruction design, enabling a more rigorous and fine-grained assessment of how well MLLMs align their outputs with both visual input and textual instructions. Furthermore, by fine-tuning MLLMs on our dataset, we achieve substantial gains in visual instruction-following accuracy and adherence. Through extensive evaluation across representative MLLMs, we provide new insights into the strengths and limitations of current models.
tldr: This paper introduces VC-IFEval, a new benchmark dataset for evaluating the visual instruction-following capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), showing improved performance after fine-tuning.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为 VC-IFEval 的新基准数据集，用于评估多模态大型语言模型 (MLLM) 的视觉指令跟随能力，并在微调后显示出性能提升。
summary_zh: 评估多模态大型语言模型（MLLM）的指令遵循（IF）能力对于严格评估模型输出是否忠实地遵循用户指定的意图至关重要。然而，现有评估MLLM指令遵循能力的基准测试主要侧重于文本模态下的口头指令。这些局限性阻碍了对指令遵循能力的全面分析，因为它们忽略了语义丰富的视觉模态中隐含的约束。为了弥补这一差距，我们引入了VC-IFEval，这是一个新的基准测试，同时提供了一个系统构建的数据集，用于评估MLLM在多模态设置下的指令遵循能力。我们的基准测试系统地将视觉相关的约束纳入指令设计中，从而能够更严格、更细粒度地评估MLLM如何将其输出与视觉输入和文本指令对齐。此外，通过在我们的数据集上微调MLLM，我们显著提高了视觉指令遵循的准确性和一致性。通过对具有代表性的MLLM进行广泛评估，我们为当前模型的优势和局限性提供了新的见解。

title: Dual-quaternion learning control for autonomous vehicle trajectory tracking with safety guarantees
summary:
We propose a learning-based trajectory tracking controller for autonomous robotic platforms whose motion can be described kinematically on $\mathrm{SE}(3)$. The controller is formulated in the dual quaternion framework and operates at the velocity level, assuming direct command of angular and linear velocities, as is standard in many aerial vehicles and omnidirectional mobile robots. Gaussian Process (GP) regression is integrated into a geometric feedback law to learn and compensate online for unknown, state-dependent disturbances and modeling imperfections affecting both attitude and position, while preserving the algebraic structure and coupling properties inherent to rigid-body motion.
  The proposed approach does not rely on explicit parametric models of the unknown effects, making it well-suited for robotic systems subject to sensor-induced disturbances, unmodeled actuation couplings, and environmental uncertainties. A Lyapunov-based analysis establishes probabilistic ultimate boundedness of the pose tracking error under bounded GP uncertainty, providing formal stability guarantees for the learning-based controller.
  Simulation results demonstrate accurate and smooth trajectory tracking in the presence of realistic, localized disturbances, including correlated rotational and translational effects arising from magnetometer perturbations. These results illustrate the potential of combining geometric modeling and probabilistic learning to achieve robust, data-efficient pose control for autonomous robotic systems.
tldr: The paper proposes a learning-based trajectory tracking controller using dual quaternions and Gaussian Processes for autonomous robots, offering probabilistic stability guarantees and robustness to disturbances. It focuses on geometric modeling and probabilistic learning for robust pose control.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种基于学习的轨迹跟踪控制器，该控制器使用双四元数和高斯过程，适用于自主机器人，并提供概率稳定性保证和抗扰动能力。 重点是几何建模和概率学习，以实现鲁棒的姿态控制。
summary_zh:
我们提出了一种基于学习的轨迹跟踪控制器，用于在$\mathrm{SE}(3)$上运动学描述的自主机器人平台。该控制器在双四元数框架下构建，并在速度层面上运行，假设可以直接控制角速度和线速度，这在许多飞行器和全向移动机器人中是标准配置。高斯过程（GP）回归被集成到一个几何反馈律中，以在线学习和补偿影响姿态和位置的未知、状态相关的扰动和建模缺陷，同时保持刚体运动固有的代数结构和耦合性质。

所提出的方法不依赖于未知效应的显式参数模型，使其非常适合于受到传感器引起的扰动、未建模的驱动耦合和环境不确定性影响的机器人系统。基于李雅普诺夫的分析建立了在有界GP不确定性下位姿跟踪误差的概率最终有界性，为基于学习的控制器提供了形式化的稳定性保证。

仿真结果表明，在存在真实的、局部扰动（包括由磁力计扰动引起的相关旋转和平移效应）的情况下，轨迹跟踪是准确且平滑的。这些结果表明了结合几何建模和概率学习来实现自主机器人系统鲁棒、数据高效的位姿控制的潜力。

title: HEXAR: a Hierarchical Explainability Architecture for Robots
summary: As robotic systems become increasingly complex, the need for explainable decision-making becomes critical. Existing explainability approaches in robotics typically either focus on individual modules, which can be difficult to query from the perspective of high-level behaviour, or employ monolithic approaches, which do not exploit the modularity of robotic architectures. We present HEXAR (Hierarchical EXplainability Architecture for Robots), a novel framework that provides a plug-in, hierarchical approach to generate explanations about robotic systems. HEXAR consists of specialised component explainers using diverse explanation techniques (e.g., LLM-based reasoning, causal models, feature importance, etc) tailored to specific robot modules, orchestrated by an explainer selector that chooses the most appropriate one for a given query. We implement and evaluate HEXAR on a TIAGo robot performing assistive tasks in a home environment, comparing it against end-to-end and aggregated baseline approaches across 180 scenario-query variations. We observe that HEXAR significantly outperforms baselines in root cause identification, incorrect information exclusion, and runtime, offering a promising direction for transparent autonomous systems.
tldr: The paper introduces HEXAR, a hierarchical explainability framework for robots that combines specialized component explainers with an explainer selector to improve transparency and performance in robotic decision-making, demonstrating superior performance over baselines in root cause identification, information exclusion, and runtime.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为 HEXAR 的机器人分层可解释性框架，它将专门的组件解释器与解释器选择器相结合，以提高机器人决策的透明度和性能。实验表明，HEXAR 在根本原因识别、信息排除和运行时方面优于基线方法。
summary_zh: 随着机器人系统日趋复杂，对可解释决策的需求变得至关重要。现有的机器人可解释性方法通常要么侧重于单个模块，这导致难以从高层行为的角度进行查询，要么采用单体方法，而这未能利用机器人架构的模块化特性。我们提出 HEXAR（机器人分层可解释性架构），这是一个新颖的框架，提供一种插件式的分层方法来生成关于机器人系统的解释。HEXAR 包含使用各种解释技术（例如，基于 LLM 的推理、因果模型、特征重要性等）的专用组件解释器，这些技术针对特定的机器人模块量身定制，并通过解释器选择器进行编排，以便为给定的查询选择最合适的解释器。我们在执行家庭环境辅助任务的 TIAGo 机器人上实施并评估了 HEXAR，将其与端到端和聚合基线方法在 180 种场景-查询变化中进行了比较。我们观察到，在根本原因识别、不正确信息排除和运行时方面，HEXAR 显著优于基线方法，为透明自主系统提供了一个有前景的方向。

title: PiDR: Physics-Informed Inertial Dead Reckoning for Autonomous Platforms
summary: A fundamental requirement for full autonomy is the ability to sustain accurate navigation in the absence of external data, such as GNSS signals or visual information. In these challenging environments, the platform must rely exclusively on inertial sensors, leading to pure inertial navigation. However, the inherent noise and other error terms of the inertial sensors in such real-world scenarios will cause the navigation solution to drift over time. Although conventional deep-learning models have emerged as a possible approach to inertial navigation, they are inherently black-box in nature. Furthermore, they struggle to learn effectively with limited supervised sensor data and often fail to preserve physical principles. To address these limitations, we propose PiDR, a physics-informed inertial dead-reckoning framework for autonomous platforms in situations of pure inertial navigation. PiDR offers transparency by explicitly integrating inertial navigation principles into the network training process through the physics-informed residual component. PiDR plays a crucial role in mitigating abrupt trajectory deviations even under limited or sparse supervision. We evaluated PiDR on real-world datasets collected by a mobile robot and an autonomous underwater vehicle. We obtained more than 29% positioning improvement in both datasets, demonstrating the ability of PiDR to generalize different platforms operating in various environments and dynamics. Thus, PiDR offers a robust, lightweight, yet effective architecture and can be deployed on resource-constrained platforms, enabling real-time pure inertial navigation in adverse scenarios.
tldr: The paper introduces PiDR, a physics-informed deep learning framework for inertial dead reckoning that improves positioning accuracy in autonomous platforms by integrating inertial navigation principles into the network training process. It demonstrates improved performance on real-world datasets over traditional methods.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了PiDR，一个基于物理信息的深度学习惯性航迹推算框架，通过将惯性导航原理融入网络训练过程，提高了自主平台上的定位精度。实验表明，在真实数据集上，其性能优于传统方法。
summary_zh: 完全自主性的一个基本要求是在缺乏外部数据的情况下，如GNSS信号或视觉信息，维持精准导航的能力。在这些具有挑战性的环境中，平台必须完全依赖惯性传感器，从而进行纯惯性导航。然而，在这种真实场景中，惯性传感器固有的噪声和其他误差项会导致导航解随时间漂移。尽管传统的深度学习模型已成为惯性导航的一种可能方法，但它们本质上是黑盒。此外，它们难以在有限的监督传感器数据下进行有效学习，并且常常无法保留物理原理。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出PiDR，一种面向纯惯性导航情况下自主平台的物理信息惯性航位推算框架。 PiDR通过将惯性导航原理显式地整合到网络训练过程中，并通过物理信息残差组件来实现透明性。即使在有限或稀疏的监督下，PiDR也能在减轻突发轨迹偏差方面发挥关键作用。我们在移动机器人和自主水下航行器采集的真实世界数据集上评估了PiDR。在两个数据集中，我们都获得了超过29%的定位精度提升，证明了PiDR能够推广到在不同环境和动态中运行的不同平台。因此，PiDR提供了一种稳健、轻量且有效的架构，可以部署在资源受限的平台上，从而在恶劣场景中实现实时的纯惯性导航。

title: Validating Generalist Robots with Situation Calculus and STL Falsification
summary: Generalist robots are becoming a reality, capable of interpreting natural language instructions and executing diverse operations. However, their validation remains challenging because each task induces its own operational context and correctness specification, exceeding the assumptions of traditional validation methods. We propose a two-layer validation framework that combines abstract reasoning with concrete system falsification. At the abstract layer, situation calculus models the world and derives weakest preconditions, enabling constraint-aware combinatorial testing to systematically generate diverse, semantically valid world-task configurations with controllable coverage strength. At the concrete layer, these configurations are instantiated for simulation-based falsification with STL monitoring. Experiments on tabletop manipulation tasks show that our framework effectively uncovers failure cases in the NVIDIA GR00T controller, demonstrating its promise for validating general-purpose robot autonomy.
tldr: The paper introduces a two-layer validation framework combining situation calculus and STL falsification to validate generalist robots, effectively uncovering failure cases in the NVIDIA GR00T controller.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种结合情景演算和STL伪造的两层验证框架，用于验证通用机器人，有效地发现了NVIDIA GR00T控制器的故障。
summary_zh: 通用型机器人正逐渐成为现实，它们能够理解自然语言指令并执行多样化的操作。然而，它们的验证仍然充满挑战，因为每个任务都会引入其自身的操作环境和正确性规范，超出了传统验证方法的假设。我们提出了一种双层验证框架，将抽象推理与具体的系统证伪相结合。在抽象层，情景演算对世界进行建模并推导出最弱前提条件，从而实现约束感知的组合测试，以系统地生成具有可控覆盖强度的、多样化的、语义上有效的世界-任务配置。在具体层，这些配置被实例化，用于基于仿真的、使用STL监控的证伪。在桌面操作任务上的实验表明，我们的框架有效地发现了NVIDIA GR00T控制器的故障案例，证明了其在验证通用机器人自主能力方面的潜力。

title: A Bi-directional Adaptive Framework for Agile UAV Landing
summary: Autonomous landing on mobile platforms is crucial for extending quadcopter operational flexibility, yet conventional methods are often too inefficient for highly dynamic scenarios. The core limitation lies in the prevalent ``track-then-descend'' paradigm, which treats the platform as a passive target and forces the quadcopter to perform complex, sequential maneuvers. This paper challenges that paradigm by introducing a bi-directional cooperative landing framework that redefines the roles of the vehicle and the platform. The essential innovation is transforming the problem from a single-agent tracking challenge into a coupled system optimization. Our key insight is that the mobile platform is not merely a target, but an active agent in the landing process. It proactively tilts its surface to create an optimal, stable terminal attitude for the approaching quadcopter. This active cooperation fundamentally breaks the sequential model by parallelizing the alignment and descent phases. Concurrently, the quadcopter's planning pipeline focuses on generating a time-optimal and dynamically feasible trajectory that minimizes energy consumption. This bi-directional coordination allows the system to execute the recovery in an agile manner, characterized by aggressive trajectory tracking and rapid state synchronization within transient windows. The framework's effectiveness, validated in dynamic scenarios, significantly improves the efficiency, precision, and robustness of autonomous quadrotor recovery in complex and time-constrained missions.
tldr: This paper introduces a bi-directional cooperative framework for agile UAV landing on mobile platforms, where the platform actively tilts to aid the quadcopter's descent, enabling faster and more efficient landings.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种用于敏捷无人机在移动平台上着陆的双向协同框架，其中平台主动倾斜以辅助四轴飞行器的下降，从而实现更快、更高效的着陆。
summary_zh: 在移动平台上自主着陆对于扩展四旋翼飞行器的操作灵活性至关重要，然而传统方法通常对于高度动态的场景而言效率太低。核心限制在于普遍存在的“跟踪-然后-下降”范式，该范式将平台视为被动目标，并迫使四旋翼飞行器执行复杂、连续的动作。本文挑战了这一范式，提出了一种双向协作着陆框架，该框架重新定义了飞行器和平台的作用。其本质创新是将问题从单智能体跟踪挑战转变为耦合系统优化。我们的关键洞察在于移动平台不仅仅是一个目标，而是着陆过程中的一个主动智能体。它主动倾斜其表面，为接近的四旋翼飞行器创造最佳、稳定的终端姿态。这种主动合作从根本上打破了串行模型，通过并行化对准和下降阶段。同时，四旋翼飞行器的规划流程侧重于生成一个时间最优且动态可行的轨迹，从而最大限度地减少能量消耗。这种双向协调使系统能够以敏捷的方式执行恢复，其特点是在瞬态窗口内进行积极的轨迹跟踪和快速状态同步。该框架的有效性已在动态场景中得到验证，显著提高了在复杂和时间受限任务中自主四旋翼飞行器恢复的效率、精度和鲁棒性。

title: Parameter-Robust MPPI for Safe Online Learning of Unknown Parameters
summary: Robots deployed in dynamic environments must remain safe even when key physical parameters are uncertain or change over time. We propose Parameter-Robust Model Predictive Path Integral (PRMPPI) control, a framework that integrates online parameter learning with probabilistic safety constraints. PRMPPI maintains a particle-based belief over parameters via Stein Variational Gradient Descent, evaluates safety constraints using Conformal Prediction, and optimizes both a nominal performance-driven and a safety-focused backup trajectory in parallel. This yields a controller that is cautious at first, improves performance as parameters are learned, and ensures safety throughout. Simulation and hardware experiments demonstrate higher success rates, lower tracking error, and more accurate parameter estimates than baselines.
tldr: The paper presents Parameter-Robust Model Predictive Path Integral (PRMPPI) control, a framework that integrates online parameter learning with probabilistic safety constraints for robots in dynamic environments with uncertain parameters. It uses SVGD for parameter estimation, Conformal Prediction for safety, and demonstrates improved performance and safety compared to baselines.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了参数鲁棒模型预测路径积分（PRMPPI）控制框架，该框架将在线参数学习与概率安全约束相结合，用于解决动态环境中参数不确定的机器人控制问题。PRMPPI使用Stein变分梯度下降进行参数估计，利用Conformal Prediction进行安全评估，实验表明其性能和安全性优于基线方法。
summary_zh: 在动态环境中部署的机器人，即使关键物理参数不确定或随时间变化，也必须保持安全。我们提出参数鲁棒模型预测路径积分(PRMPPI)控制，该框架集成了在线参数学习与概率安全性约束。 PRMPPI 通过 Stein 变分梯度下降维护基于粒子的参数置信度，使用共形预测评估安全性约束，并并行优化一个标称的性能驱动轨迹和一个以安全为中心的备用轨迹。这产生了一个起初谨慎的控制器，随着参数的学习提高性能，并始终确保安全。模拟和硬件实验表明，相比基线方法，该方法具有更高的成功率、更低的跟踪误差和更准确的参数估计。

title: Soft Responsive Materials Enhance Humanoid Safety
summary: Humanoid robots are envisioned as general-purpose platforms in human-centered environments, yet their deployment is limited by vulnerability to falls and the risks posed by rigid metal-plastic structures to people and surroundings. We introduce a soft-rigid co-design framework that leverages non-Newtonian fluid-based soft responsive materials to enhance humanoid safety. The material remains compliant during normal interaction but rapidly stiffens under impact, absorbing and dissipating fall-induced forces. Physics-based simulations guide protector placement and thickness and enable learning of active fall policies. Applied to a 42 kg life-size humanoid, the protector markedly reduces peak impact and allows repeated falls without hardware damage, including drops from 3 m and tumbles down long staircases. Across diverse scenarios, the approach improves robot robustness and environmental safety. By uniting responsive materials, structural co-design, and learning-based control, this work advances interact-safe, industry-ready humanoid robots.
tldr: This paper introduces a soft-rigid co-design framework using non-Newtonian fluid-based materials to enhance humanoid robot safety during falls, demonstrating significant impact reduction and hardware protection through simulation and experimental validation.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种利用非牛顿流体材料的软硬协同设计框架，以提高人形机器人在跌倒时的安全性。通过模拟和实验验证，该框架显著降低了冲击力并保护了硬件。
summary_zh: 人形机器人被设想为以人为中心环境中的通用平台，但其部署受到跌倒的脆弱性以及刚性金属-塑料结构对人和环境造成的风险的限制。我们介绍了一种软-硬协同设计框架，该框架利用基于非牛顿流体的软响应材料来增强人形机器人的安全性。该材料在正常交互过程中保持柔顺，但在受到冲击时迅速硬化，吸收和耗散跌倒引起的力。基于物理的仿真指导保护器的放置和厚度，并支持学习主动跌倒策略。应用于一个 42 公斤的真人大小的人形机器人，该保护器显著降低了峰值冲击力，并允许重复跌倒而不会损坏硬件，包括从 3 米高处跌落和沿着长楼梯翻滚。在各种不同的场景中，该方法提高了机器人的鲁棒性和环境安全性。通过结合响应材料、结构协同设计和基于学习的控制，这项工作推进了交互安全、可用于工业的人形机器人。

title: M-SEVIQ: A Multi-band Stereo Event Visual-Inertial Quadruped-based Dataset for Perception under Rapid Motion and Challenging Illumination
summary: Agile locomotion in legged robots poses significant challenges for visual perception. Traditional frame-based cameras often fail in these scenarios for producing blurred images, particularly under low-light conditions. In contrast, event cameras capture changes in brightness asynchronously, offering low latency, high temporal resolution, and high dynamic range. These advantages make them suitable for robust perception during rapid motion and under challenging illumination. However, existing event camera datasets exhibit limitations in stereo configurations and multi-band sensing domains under various illumination conditions. To address this gap, we present M-SEVIQ, a multi-band stereo event visual and inertial quadruped dataset collected using a Unitree Go2 equipped with stereo event cameras, a frame-based camera, an inertial measurement unit (IMU), and joint encoders. This dataset contains more than 30 real-world sequences captured across different velocity levels, illumination wavelengths, and lighting conditions. In addition, comprehensive calibration data, including intrinsic, extrinsic, and temporal alignments, are provided to facilitate accurate sensor fusion and benchmarking. Our M-SEVIQ can be used to support research in agile robot perception, sensor fusion, semantic segmentation and multi-modal vision in challenging environments.
tldr: The paper introduces M-SEVIQ, a new multi-band stereo event visual-inertial quadruped dataset designed for perception research in agile robotics under challenging illumination and rapid motion conditions, addressing limitations in existing datasets.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了M-SEVIQ，一个新的多波段立体事件视觉惯性四足机器人数据集，专为在照明和快速运动条件下进行敏捷机器人感知研究而设计，解决了现有数据集的局限性。
summary_zh: 基于腿式机器人的敏捷运动对视觉感知提出了重大挑战。 传统的基于帧的相机在这种情况下经常失效，产生模糊的图像，尤其是在低光照条件下。 相比之下，事件相机异步地捕获亮度变化，具有低延迟、高时间分辨率和高动态范围的特性。 这些优势使其适用于在快速运动和具有挑战性的光照条件下进行鲁棒感知。 然而，现有的事件相机数据集在立体视觉配置和各种光照条件下的多波段传感领域存在局限性。 为了弥补这一差距，我们提出了 M-SEVIQ，一个多波段立体视觉事件与惯性四足机器人数据集，该数据集使用配备立体事件相机、基于帧的相机、惯性测量单元 (IMU) 和关节编码器的 Unitree Go2 收集。 该数据集包含超过 30 个在不同速度级别、照明波长和光照条件下捕获的真实世界序列。 此外，还提供了全面的校准数据，包括内参、外参和时间对齐，以方便准确的传感器融合和基准测试。 我们的 M-SEVIQ 可用于支持在具有挑战性环境中的敏捷机器人感知、传感器融合、语义分割和多模态视觉方面的研究。

title: Unified Meta-Representation and Feedback Calibration for General Disturbance Estimation
summary: Precise control in modern robotic applications is always an open issue due to unknown time-varying disturbances. Existing meta-learning-based approaches require a shared representation of environmental structures, which lack flexibility for realistic non-structural disturbances. Besides, representation error and the distribution shifts can lead to heavy degradation in prediction accuracy. This work presents a generalizable disturbance estimation framework that builds on meta-learning and feedback-calibrated online adaptation. By extracting features from a finite time window of past observations, a unified representation that effectively captures general non-structural disturbances can be learned without predefined structural assumptions. The online adaptation process is subsequently calibrated by a state-feedback mechanism to attenuate the learning residual originating from the representation and generalizability limitations. Theoretical analysis shows that simultaneous convergence of both the online learning error and the disturbance estimation error can be achieved. Through the unified meta-representation, our framework effectively estimates multiple rapidly changing disturbances, as demonstrated by quadrotor flight experiments. See the project page for video, supplementary material and code: https://nonstructural-metalearn.github.io.
